note: Pre-dedup. Phase 3 will merge, score, and assign tiers.

sources.writer_profile: Miguel Pires is Portugal's most prominent independent food critic, the founding editor of Mesa Marcada since 2009 and organiser of the annual Mesa Marcada Awards — the country's most widely followed restaurant prizes. Based in Lisbon, he writes for Público's Fugas supplement and international outlets including Eater, and is the author of Lisboa à Mesa, a Lisbon restaurant guide published in three languages.

sources.commercial_conflict_note: Culinary Backstreets sells food tours in Lisbon; Célia Pedroso co-founded Eat Drink Walk food tours, now operating under CB brand.
sources.writer_profile: Célia Pedroso is a Lisbon-based freelance journalist, co-author of the Gourmand Award-winning book Eat Portugal, and Lisbon bureau chief of Culinary Backstreets. She writes about food and travel for The Guardian and Eater, and appeared on Netflix's Somebody Feed Phil. Austin Bush is a contributor and food writer who reports for CB on Lisbon's neighbourhood food scenes.

Daniela Sunde-Brown is an Australian food and travel writer who has lived in Lisbon since 2018. Her independent website Ola Daniela focuses on where locals eat in the city, with a particular interest in traditional tascas and neighbourhood restaurants that avoid the tourist circuit.

Rita Geraldes is a food writer covering Lisbon for The Infatuation, the New York-based restaurant guide known for its opinionated, non-advertorial reviews. Her Lisbon guide emphasises restaurants across price points with a focus on what the experience actually feels like rather than prestige alone.

NiT is a Portuguese digital lifestyle and food publication that runs its own annual restaurant awards (Prémios NiT) and publishes the results of the Câmara de Lisboa's formal restaurant evaluation programme, Lisboa à Prova. Eva Reimão and Catarina Simões cover the Lisbon restaurant scene for NiT with a focus on new openings and annual recognition.

sources.commercial_conflict_note: Time Out operates Time Out Market Lisboa at Mercado da Ribeira — commercial conflict of interest. Listed in SECONDARY_SOURCES.

extractions._article: Yōso, Lisboa: Uma das surpresas estreladas do Guia Michelin 2025
extractions.name: Yōso
extractions.quote: Meio escondido, próximo do Palácio das Necessidades, em Alcântara, o Yōso é um dos mais recentes exemplos de alta cozinha japonesa em Lisboa — restaurante de apenas 22 lugares com um único menu omakase com peixes da costa portuguesa, vieiras de Hokkaido e wasabi fresco.
extractions.signature_dish: omakase with Portuguese coastal fish, Hokkaido scallops, fresh wasabi

extractions.name: Canalha
extractions.quote: Portuguese fare sprinkled with a bit of Spanish inspiration from a chef who left fine dining — produce-driven bistro where you could spend €20 to €200, so everyone is welcome.

extractions.name: Galeto
extractions.quote: American diner-style snack bar on Avenida da República serving club sandwiches, burgers, and Brazilian feijoada — a Lisbon institution that has barely changed since 1966.

extractions.name: Acarajé da Carol
extractions.quote: The only place in Portugal selling authentic Bahian acarajé: fluffy white cloud of ground black-eyed peas transformed, somehow, into a crispy red fritter — Carol Alves de Brito's tiny counter is the real thing.

extractions.name: Taberna Sal Grosso
extractions.quote: Small tavern revived by new owners, focused on beer, wine, and petiscos — tightly packed cubby hole in Alfama with a chalkboard menu, well known to both tourists and locals for shrimp with garlic, oxtail, and cod tongue with pea purée.
extractions.signature_dish: cod tongue with pea purée; shrimp with garlic

extractions.name: O Velho Eurico
extractions.quote: Family-run tasca behind Rossio that helped kick off Lisbon's modern tasca craze when it opened in 2019 — chef Zé Paulo Rocha's menu features octopus lagareiro said to be the best in the city.

extractions.name: Casa de Angola
extractions.quote: Serving muamba — Angola's iconic national dish — since 1970 in Mouraria, with a newer clientele discovering this community institution and its slow-cooked chicken stew with palm oil and okra.
extractions.signature_dish: muamba de galinha (Angolan chicken stew with palm oil)

extractions.name: Cervejaria Ramiro
extractions.quote: Temple of seafood — a beer hall with fresh seafood arriving daily, where oysters, spider crab, perfectly-cooked prawns and a famous prego steak sandwich are the pillars of a Lisbon institution since the 1950s.
extractions.signature_dish: prego do lombo; spider crab; gambas à guilho

extractions.name: Café Tati
extractions.quote: A breath of fresh air at Cais do Sodré since 2011 — relaxed meals, organic wines, and occasional live music in a warm space that never feels precious or tourist-facing.

extractions.name: Petisco Saloio
extractions.quote: A tasca featuring grilled food and simple dishes that represents the future of the traditional format — unpretentious neighbourhood eating at its most honest.

extractions.name: Prado
extractions.quote: Farm-to-table pioneer in Lisbon serving small plates built around seasonal ingredients from small producers — cockles with spinach and coriander, and beef tartare with dried cabbage are among its flagship dishes.

extractions._article: Street Smarts: Campo de Ourique — Austin Bush, 2026-01-30
extractions.name: Pigmeu
extractions.quote: Chef-owner Miguel Peres orders half a pig weekly and uses every part — liver on toast is the signature petisco in this compact Campo de Ourique spot dedicated to Alentejo pork and natural wines.
extractions.signature_dish: liver on toast; Alentejo pork preparations

extractions._article: Street Smarts: Campo de Ourique — Austin Bush, 2026-01-30
extractions.name: Bichomau
extractions.quote: Cook-owner Tomás Rocha puts product first — shokupan with raw violet shrimp is the kind of precise, ingredient-led dish this small Campo de Ourique kitchen produces with quiet confidence.

extractions._article: Street Smarts: Campo de Ourique — Austin Bush, 2026-01-30
extractions.name: Imperial de Campo de Ourique
extractions.quote: Warm welcomes from owner João Gomes and hearty traditional dishes prepared by his wife Adelaide — grelhados, pratos de tacho, and the kind of cooking that makes this a real neighbourhood anchor.

extractions._article: Street Smarts: Campo de Ourique — Austin Bush, 2026-01-30
extractions.name: Afonso dos Leitões
extractions.quote: Negrais-style suckling pig is the reason to visit this Campo de Ourique specialist — slow-roasted until the skin crisps and the meat falls from the bone.

extractions._article: Best Bites of 2023 in Lisbon — Célia Pedroso, 2023-12-22
extractions.name: Antiga Camponesa
extractions.quote: Portuguese gastronomy at its core but not afraid to go in slightly different, smart, subtle, delicious directions — chef André Magalhães brings the same intellectual approach here as at Taberna da Rua das Flores.

extractions._article: Best Bites of 2023 in Lisbon — Célia Pedroso, 2023-12-22
extractions.name: Gunpowder
extractions.quote: Squid tossed in garlic, kokum, peppers and onions — bright, spicy and perfectly seasoned — chef Nirmat Save's cooking is among the most distinctive non-Portuguese food in Lisbon.
extractions.signature_dish: squid with garlic, kokum and peppers

extractions._article: Best Bites of 2023 in Lisbon — Célia Pedroso, 2023-12-22
extractions.name: Casa de Angola
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — already extracted above from city guide. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Kitaba — roasted jinguba peanuts ground with a pinch of jindungo pepper and salt — is as simple as it is delicious: Paulo Soares keeps the soul of Angolan cooking alive in this Mouraria institution since 1970.
extractions.signature_dish: kitaba (roasted peanut paste); muamba de galinha

extractions._article: Local's Lisbon Restaurant Guide (2026): 42 Best Places To Eat — Daniela Sunde-Brown, 2024-11-30
extractions.name: Ultimo Porto
extractions.quote: Best grilled fish in Lisbon — an unusual waterfront restaurant jammed up against the docks in Santos, where chocos trinchados, red mullet, and the tomato-and-onion salad are reason enough to make the trip.

extractions.name: O Tachadas
extractions.quote: T-bone steaks hacked on a chopping block and served on the bone in a Santos neighbourhood joint that plays it completely straight — no frills, just very good beef.

extractions.name: Zé da Mouraria
extractions.quote: Lunch-only Mouraria spot where family-sized portions arrive on frying pans — only open weekday lunchtimes and always packed with construction workers and neighbourhood regulars.

extractions.name: Zé dos Cornos
extractions.quote: Charcoal-grilled ribs and paper tablecloths in Mouraria — old-school, cash-only, and reliably excellent in a neighbourhood that has seen a lot of change around it.

extractions.name: Solar dos Nunes
extractions.quote: Institution open since 1988 in Alcântara serving traditional Portuguese and Alentejo cooking — the menu was designed to take you on a journey through regional flavours, with fresh ingredients and generous portions.

extractions.name: Vida de Tasca
extractions.quote: Chef Leonor Godinho's 2024 opening in Alvalade — peak tasca energy with paper tablecloths, a handwritten daily menu, and a kitchen that treats simple ingredients with serious skill.

extractions.name: Ramiro
extractions.quote: Lisbon's most famous seafood hall in Intendente — piled bowls of clams, scarlet shrimp, and spider crab, where manual crab-cracking is still the labour of love it always was. Expect a 30–90 minute wait.

extractions.name: Carvoaria Jacto
extractions.quote: Steak institution since 1953 in the Anjos area of Arroios — charcoal-grilled meat served at long communal tables, unchanged and unapologetic.

extractions.name: Ponto Final
extractions.quote: Riverside restaurant across the Tagus requiring a ferry from Cais do Sodré — panoramic views of Lisbon skyline and grilled fish done simply and well, included in every Lisbon guide worth trusting.

extractions.name: Canalha
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-culinary-backstreets. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Produce-driven Portuguese bistro in Belém where you could spend €20 to €200 — chef João Rodrigues left the Michelin-starred Feitoria to open this more accessible, market-driven restaurant.

extractions.name: O Velho Eurico
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-culinary-backstreets. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: One of Lisbon's hardest reservations — chef Zé Paulo Rocha opened this neo-tasca in Mouraria in 2019 with a slate menu of octopus lagareiro, piglet empanada, and leite-creme that became the template for a generation of Lisbon restaurants.

extractions.name: Taberna Sal Grosso
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-culinary-backstreets. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Tuna and fried quail are among the specialties at this tightly packed Alfama cubby hole — chalkboard menu, excellent natural wines, and prices that still feel fair despite the fame.

extractions.name: Corrupio
extractions.quote: Central bar, excellent octopus salad, and a rotating menu of small plates at Cais do Sodré — casual enough to drop in without a booking, serious enough to make you stay longer than planned.

extractions.name: Obra
extractions.quote: Prawn brioche — a sandwich stuffed with creamy camarão — is the dish everyone orders first at this Santos spot, where the kitchen keeps things simple and ingredient-led.

extractions.name: Taberna da Rua das Flores
extractions.quote: Modern tavern movement pioneer in Chiado — chef André Magalhães opened this small, celebrated spot that launched a generation of Lisbon neo-tascas, with top-quality local produce and a short daily menu.

extractions.name: ZunZum Gastrobar
extractions.quote: Chef Marlene Vieira — former MasterChef Portugal judge — runs this relaxed fine dining restaurant near the cruise terminal, with shellfish rice, filhós with cockles in garlicky sauce, and a famous francesinha.

extractions.name: Brilhante
extractions.quote: Red-velvet, gold and leather-clad diner at Cais do Sodré with a wine list that punches above the neighbourhood and small plates that reward careful ordering.

extractions.name: Taberna Albricoque
extractions.quote: Algarve-inspired cockle pastries and a short menu of southern Portuguese dishes served in a warm, informal setting near Santa Apolónia station — a neighbourhood restaurant that has quietly become a must.

extractions.name: Pigmeu
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-culinary-backstreets (CB Campo de Ourique). Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Nose-to-tail pig concept in Campo de Ourique — the kitchen orders half a pig weekly and uses every part, with a Bib Gourmand from Michelin and a wine list of naturals to match.

extractions.name: O Frade
extractions.quote: Michelin Bib Gourmand in Belém, known for its arrozes — rice dishes cooked to order using ingredients from small producers, with a focused wine list and a relaxed atmosphere.

extractions.name: Rosetta
extractions.quote: Tiny produce-driven share-plate restaurant in Bairro Alto with grilled peaches with burrata, clams with beurre blanc, and a daily menu that changes with the market.
extractions.signature_dish: grilled peaches with burrata; clams with beurre blanc

extractions.name: Tozzi
extractions.quote: Long-fermented pizza crusts near Saldanha — the dough rests for 48 hours before baking, producing a light, blistered base that stands out in Lisbon's growing pizza scene.

extractions.name: BouBou's
extractions.quote: Chef Louise Bourrat — winner of French TV show Top Chef — brought off-beat fine dining to Príncipe Real with a tasting menu that blends French precision with Lisbon irreverence. Note: chef left in April 2026.
extractions._flag: open_status_check: chef change announced April 2026 (Mesa Marcada); verify current concept before publication

extractions.name: Vibe
extractions.quote: Chef Mattia Stanchieri's Michelin-recommended restaurant in Chiado — no fixed menu, customers choose by course count (5 or 7), with a Thai-influenced chapter that signals an ambition beyond borders.

extractions.name: Guelra
extractions.quote: Fish-focused diner in Belém where bacalhau³ — salted cod prepared three ways — is the centrepiece, alongside a short menu of Portuguese coastal fish treated with minimal interference.

extractions.name: Bar Alimentar
extractions.quote: Collaboration between the chef behind Tricky's and bartenders from Imprensa cocktail bar — modern share plates combining Italian and Portuguese flavours in a minimalist Príncipe Real space that feels like an after-party.
extractions.signature_dish: whipped cod with caramelised onion cannoli

extractions.name: Zula Bistro
extractions.quote: Middle Eastern offerings in Santos — hummus, grilled meats, and mezze-style sharing dishes in a warm, neighbourhood-facing space that has built a loyal local following.

extractions.name: Kefi Greek Bistro
extractions.quote: Mediterranean cooking with Greek roots in Estrela — a neighbourhood bistro that brings feta, olive oil, and grilled octopus to a corner of Lisbon that needed a reliable casual dinner option.

extractions.name: Bom Bom Bom
extractions._geo_note: Graça — not in GEOGRAPHIC_BOUNDARY_INCLUDE; null neighbourhood assigned
extractions.quote: Vinyl bar in Graça with seasonal snacks and a rotating selection of natural wines — a social spot more bar than restaurant, with occasional live music.
extractions._flag: rec-remove candidate: vinyl bar with snacks — may not qualify as restaurant

extractions.name: Hachi Kare-Ya
extractions.quote: Japanese curry specialist near Anjos station — a single-dish concept that has quietly become one of the most talked-about cheap eats in Lisbon's Arroios neighbourhood.

extractions.name: Taberna do Mar
extractions._geo_note: Graça — not in GEOGRAPHIC_BOUNDARY_INCLUDE; null neighbourhood assigned
extractions.quote: Grilled sardine nigiri is the signature dish of this Graça spot that mixes Japanese and Portuguese cuisines — one of the most original combinations in Lisbon, with a short rotating menu.

extractions._article: The 29 Best Restaurants In Lisbon — Rita Geraldes, 2023-07-24
extractions.name: Capsule Neo Bistro
extractions.quote: Low-key fancy without torching your credit card — katsu chicken sandwich and crispy potato pancakes make this Baixa bistro one of Lisbon's most pleasant surprises for a casual lunch.
extractions.signature_dish: katsu chicken sandwich; crispy potato pancakes

extractions.name: Santa Joana
extractions.quote: Grand dining room with soaring ceilings and pink marble tables — grilled chicken hearts, chickpea panisse, and shrimp rice with lobster in a setting that feels genuinely grand without feeling stiff.
extractions.signature_dish: grilled chicken hearts; shrimp rice with lobster

extractions.name: O Velho Eurico
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-culinary-backstreets and src-ola-daniela. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Small spot on the corner of a sunny square near St George's Castle — piglet empanada on the daily menu scribbled on slate, with chef Zé Paulo Rocha's neo-tasca still the hardest booking in the city.

extractions.name: Encanto
extractions.quote: Fine dining without the fuss — Jerusalem artichokes, fire-kissed sweet potatoes, and a meatless cozido at José Avillez's Chiado restaurant that earned a Michelin Green Star for sustainable cuisine.

extractions.name: Canalha
extractions.quote: Phenomenal shareable plates without any pretentiousness — smoked eel, two-year-aged tuna tiradito, and the prato do dia make Canalha in Belém the restaurant Lisbon needed.

extractions.name: Prado
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-culinary-backstreets. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Farm-to-table pioneer in Lisbon's Sé district — beef tartare with dried cabbage, smoked eel with caramelised onion rice, and mushroom ice cream on a menu built around small producers.
extractions.signature_dish: beef tartare with dried cabbage; smoked eel with caramelised onion rice

extractions.name: Sem
extractions.quote: Seven-course tasting menu with a zero-waste ethos — honey-roasted carrots with pomegranate, razor clam escabeche, and sophisticated delicate cooking from former alumni of Silo in London.
extractions.signature_dish: razor clam escabeche; honey-roasted carrots with pomegranate

extractions.name: Cervejaria Ramiro
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-culinary-backstreets and src-ola-daniela. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Most important seafood restaurant in the city — stuffed crab, gambas à guilho, perfectly-cooked prawns, and their famous prego do lombo steak sandwich make this Intendente institution unmissable.
extractions.signature_dish: gambas à guilho; stuffed crab; prego do lombo

extractions.name: Skizzo
extractions.quote: Ticks all the boxes of how to open a hip Lisbon restaurant — flatbread with arugula and stracciatella, buttery-charred scallops, and lobster à guilho in a Lapa space with smart design and easy prices.

extractions.name: Bar Alimentar
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-ola-daniela. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Feels like an after-party at the house of a minimalist artist — whipped cod with caramelised onion cannoli and fava beans vignarola in a Príncipe Real space that has been one of the city's best openings.
extractions.signature_dish: whipped cod with caramelised onion cannoli; fava beans vignarola

extractions.name: Pap'Açôrda
extractions.quote: Synonymous with Lisbon nightlife — peixinhos da horta, ameijoas à bulhão pato, and açorda at Cais do Sodré, in a restaurant that has managed to stay genuinely good despite decades of fame.

extractions.name: O Maravilhas
extractions.quote: Traditional Portuguese spot in Alcântara with big portions — scrambled eggs with farinheira, cod fritters, fried green beans, and serradura dessert at prices that feel like a throwback.

extractions.name: Tasca Pete
extractions._geo_note: Graça/Penha de França — not in GEOGRAPHIC_BOUNDARY_INCLUDE; null neighbourhood
extractions.quote: Feels a bit like going to a friend's place for dinner — blueberry focaccia, potato terrine, and a short daily menu that Pete changes based on what arrived from the market that morning.

extractions.name: Sem Palavras
extractions.quote: Traditional beer house inside Mercado de Alvalade — farinheira with scrambled eggs, clams with coriander and garlic, and cod dishes served at communal tables among local shoppers and market vendors.
extractions.signature_dish: farinheira with scrambled eggs; clams with coriander

extractions.name: Tico Tico
extractions.quote: Slightly off the tourist radar in Alvalade — meat croquettes, lamprey rice, polvo à lagareiro, Portuguese feijoada, and prego sandwich in one of the most traditional beer and seafood restaurants in the city.

extractions.name: Essencial
extractions.quote: Feels more like having dinner inside someone's house in Bairro Alto — oven-baked rice with scarlet shrimp, turbot with caviar, and pigeon foie gras in an intimate 30-seat room with no sign outside.
extractions.signature_dish: oven-baked rice with scarlet shrimp; turbot with caviar

extractions.name: Ultimo Porto
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-ola-daniela. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Located in the middle of the shipping containers at Alcântara docks — chocos trinchados, red mullet fish, and tomato and onion salad served to a loyal crowd of locals who know what they're doing.

extractions.name: A Taberna do Mar
extractions._note: DUPLICATE of Taberna do Mar in src-ola-daniela (null neighbourhood). Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Mixes Japanese and Portuguese cuisines — grilled sardine nigiri and sardine flan in a Graça restaurant that handles fusion without condescension, keeping Portuguese fish front and centre.

extractions.name: Belcanto
extractions.quote: José Avillez's crown jewel — two Michelin stars and ranked 25th on the World's 50 Best list, with Classics and Landscapes tasting menus from €235 that showcase Portuguese ingredients at their most elevated.
extractions.signature_dish: lobster with white beans, marrow and caviar

extractions.name: Vibe
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-ola-daniela. Merge in Phase 3 — language_pool will be 'both' (PT + EN, different publishers).
extractions.quote: Não há ementa tradicional — os clientes selecionam pelo número de pratos, com menus de 5 ou 7 cursos, sob o comando do chef Mattia Stanchieri, que conquistou recomendação Michelin em 2025.

extractions.name: À Costa
extractions.quote: Chef Olivier da Costa juntou o lingueirão, o berbigão, a amêijoa e outros tesouros do mar português num restaurante de doca com vista para o Tejo, distinguido Melhor Abertura do Ano nos TheFork Awards 2024.

extractions._article: The Best Restaurants in Lisbon — Time Out editorial, 2026-02-11
extractions.name: Belcanto
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-infatuation. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Two Michelin stars and ranked 25th on the 50 Best list — José Avillez's Classics and Landscapes tasting menus from €235 are the benchmark for contemporary Portuguese fine dining.

extractions.name: O Velho Eurico
extractions.quote: Menu scribbled on slate near St George's Castle — liver and onions, octopus lagareiro, chicken cabidela in shareable portions for €20 per person, with a leite-creme that is worth the visit alone.

extractions.name: Ryoshi
extractions.quote: Dark, sultry Cais do Sodré restaurant with inventive dishes — mackerel on silky tofu, grilled eel with seasoned rice and egg yolk, fried dough — and a soundtrack of electronic dance music that suits the mood.
extractions.signature_dish: mackerel on tofu; grilled eel with rice and egg yolk

extractions.name: Arkhe
extractions.quote: Michelin-starred vegetarian tasting menu in Chiado — chef João Ricardo Alves serves seasonal dishes like focaccia with smoked butter, cauliflower with green apple and hazelnut, earned a star in 2025.
extractions.signature_dish: cauliflower with green apple and hazelnut

extractions.name: Cervejaria Ramiro
extractions.quote: Oysters, spider crab, perfectly-cooked prawns, and the famous prego do lombo steak sandwich — Lisbon's most celebrated seafood hall since the 1950s is still worth every minute of the queue.

extractions.name: Zunzum Gastrobar
extractions._note: DUPLICATE — also in src-ola-daniela. Merge in Phase 3.
extractions.quote: Chef Marlene Vieira's relaxed fine dining near the cruise terminal — filhós with cockles in garlicky sauce, shellfish rice, roast beef sandwich, and a famous francesinha that draws a loyal following.

extractions.name: Prado
extractions.quote: Nordic farm-to-table in Lisbon's Sé district — António Galapito's flagship dish of cockles with spinach, coriander and toasted bread, using ingredients from small producers.
extractions.signature_dish: cockles with spinach, coriander and toasted bread

extractions.name: Gancho
extractions.quote: Chef Louise Bourrat's new venture in Alfama — chicken-cabidela arancini, beef tartare à Brás, and pointed cabbage with spicy sauce in a vibrant neo-tasca setting where two cultures meet without apology.
extractions.signature_dish: chicken-cabidela arancini; beef tartare à Brás

extractions.name: Familjen
extractions.quote: Chef Petter Nyström's short Scandinavian menu in Lisbon — sashimi with lemongrass and caesar salad with pistachios and pickled rose petals in a quietly confident room that rewards repeat visits.

extractions.name: Dom Feijão
extractions.quote: Near Avenida de Roma in Alvalade — fish stew, octopus lagareiro-style, excellent grilled meats and fish, and lamprey from Alto Minho in season, with cozido à portuguesa on Wednesdays and Saturdays.

extractions.name: SEM
extractions.quote: Seven-course tasting menu at €70 with zero waste ethos — sophisticated, delicate dining cooked and seasoned to order by the alumni of Silo London; one of its last nights hosted Douglas McMaster himself.
extractions._flag: open_status_check: Mesa Marcada reported April 2026 as among SEM's last nights — verify current status

extractions.name: As Bifanas do Afonso
extractions.quote: 50 years serving traditional bifanas with thin-sliced pork tossed in white wine, lard and garlic sauce — Lisbon's most beloved pork sandwich, drawing lunchtime queues of locals who know better.
extractions.signature_dish: bifana (pork sandwich with white wine and garlic)

extractions.name: Sala de Corte
extractions.quote: Dark wood, low lighting, slick open kitchen with an aging cabinet displaying 900-gram T-bones and chuletón — the most serious dry-aged steakhouse in Lisbon, for those who know what they want.
